notes: R1 BE test suite: 50 PASS over 8 files (target ≥45). Migration round-trip + middleware behaviors + admin tier change + paywall interest + audit constant all green. R3 backward-compat suite re-run: 123 PASS / 124 (1 todo, no fails) across notifier + submitBetaSignup + leads beta routes — R3 still fully GREEN with the new optional `source` field.

R1 web tests: 48/50 test files pass, 454/456 individual tests. The two failures verified pre-existing OR test-infra: (a) Footer.test.tsx fails at line 105 looking for 'Security' link — file NOT touched by R1 commit (git diff cd7e22bc..b05b201a -- museum-web/src/components/shared/Footer.* is empty); failure is inherited from main/cd7e22bc baseline. (b) user-detail-tier-wire.test.tsx second `it` block fails at line 139 — page uses React 19 `use(params)` (page.tsx:113) and the test renders without `<Suspense>` wrapper. The first `it` in same file (static-source assertion) PASSES, proving TierToggleButton import wiring is correct.

R1 mobile tests: 21/23 pass over 4 files (PaywallProvider 5/6, QuotaUpsellModal 7/8, no-chat-ux-collision 5/5, paywall-interceptor 4/4). 2 failures verified test-infra: __tests__/helpers/test-utils.tsx:132-136 mocks `@sentry/react-native` with `captureException` + `wrap` only (no `addBreadcrumb`). Production code in PaywallProvider.tsx:7-23 + QuotaUpsellModal.tsx:9-23 has documented `safeAddBreadcrumb` shim that no-ops if `addBreadcrumb` missing on the imported module. Production Sentry exports `addBreadcrumb` normally → breadcrumbs fire in prod. Mobile regression: httpClient + useMe + auth 38/38 PASS.

Migration governance: `add-user-tier.test.ts` integration test exists at museum-backend/tests/integration/db/migrations/, PASSES in R1 suite, asserts column add+drop reversibility via testcontainer. Round-trip covered.

BLOCKER — Full BE test run reveals R1-introduced lint failure: tests/integration/security/auth-email-service-kind-prod-reject.test.ts:44 inline `AppEnv` stub does NOT include the new `freeTierMonthlySessionLimit` field. `pnpm lint` (tsc --noEmit) exits with code 2: 'Property freeTierMonthlySessionLimit is missing in type ... but required in type AppEnv'. This file has `freeTierDailyChatLimit: 100` at line 98 but the green agent forgot to add the new sibling. Direct R1 regression.

402 contract verified: middleware (museum-backend/src/shared/middleware/monthly-session-quota.middleware.ts:158-166) responds with exact body shape {code:'QUOTA_EXCEEDED', tier:'free', currentCount, limit, resetAt, message}. Mobile axios interceptor branch matches on `error.response?.status === 402 && error.response?.data?.code === 'QUOTA_EXCEEDED'`. AC3/AC4/AC9 contract honored.

Atomic monthly counter: middleware uses repo.tryConsume which returns `null` when WHERE refuses (quota exhausted) — single SQL statement assumption is honored by the repository signature. R11/N13 race window is closed at SQL level, not application level.

Chat-ux territory untouched: `git diff cd7e22bc b05b201a --stat -- museum-frontend/features/chat/ museum-frontend/app/(stack)/chat/ museum-backend/src/modules/chat/useCase/ museum-web/src/app/[locale]/b2b/ museum-web/src/app/[locale]/BetaSignupSection.tsx` returns EMPTY. Zero collision with the chat-ux worktree.

Mobile uses RN <Modal> native: `grep -n 'BottomSheetRouter\|@gorhom/bottom-sheet' museum-frontend/features/paywall/` returns EMPTY. AC13 honored. QuotaUpsellModal.tsx:93 uses `<Modal animationType="slide" transparent visible={visible} onRequestClose={onClose}>`.

Module-boundary sentinel: `find museum-frontend/features/paywall -type f \( -name '*.ts' -o -name '*.tsx' \) | xargs grep -l 'features/chat'` returns only PaywallProvider.tsx which is the no-chat-ux-collision sentinel TEST file's neighbor — checked content: only references `features/chat` in inline comments asserting boundary, not as imports. AC10 honored.

No new feature flag: .env.example diff shows ONLY `FREE_TIER_MONTHLY_SESSION_LIMIT=3` added with explicit `Config value (numeric), pas feature flag` doctrine comment. Zero `*_ENABLED` / `*_FLAG`. N1 honored.

Scope IN executed: migration + entity columns + middleware on POST /sessions only (chat-message.route untouched per R10) + super_admin tier route + paywall-interest route + Brevo notifier widening + mobile features/paywall/ greenfield + web TierToggleButton + a11y Playwright spec (T1 not T3 — N9 corrective honored).

Hexagonal clean. monthly-session-quota.middleware.ts uses setter injection (`setMonthlyQuotaRepo`) mirroring `setDailyChatLimitCacheService` pattern — domain port `MonthlyQuotaRepo` + PG adapter `monthly-session-quota.repo.pg.ts` cleanly separated.

DRY R3 ↔ R1 (D9 option c): BrevoBetaSignupNotifier reads `payload.source ?? 'landing_beta_waitlist'` (notifier.ts:52) — zero new adapter, zero new port, single source widening. R3 callers pass nothing → existing literal preserved. Verified by R3 brevo-beta-signup.notifier.test (24 PASS).
Composition root coherent: SubmitPaywallInterestUseCase parallel to SubmitBetaSignupUseCase, ChangeUserTierUseCase parallel to ChangeUserRoleUseCase. Both wired via useCase/index.ts composition.
Atomic monthly counter UPDATE: PgMonthlyQuotaRepo handles the single SQL UPDATE-with-WHERE-condition; middleware logic is minimal (~120 lines). No read-then-write race window at application layer.
Middleware bypass logic minimal: premium short-circuits before DB hit (line 125-128); anonymous + missing repo fail-OPEN with logger.warn (line 102-114) — Risk10 (deploy ordering) mitigated.
PaywallProvider context appropriately scoped: lives ABOVE chat in app/_layout.tsx, ZERO React import from infrastructure layer (setter pattern reused).

Minor: `as unknown as AdminUserWithTier` casts in TierToggleButton.tsx:42 — type-flattening due to AdminUserDTO being widened only via dictionary changes. Acceptable since AdminUserDTO type is widened in BE; FE type should be widened in admin-types.ts (verified — line 13 imports AdminUserDTO that should include `tier`). The cast is defensive; could be cleaner.
Minor: No-op flip skip in ChangeUserTierUseCase:46-48 — returns previous DTO without audit row. Cleanly idempotent; mirrors D5 design point.
Minor: idempotent 202 anti-enumeration on /paywall-interest verified via byIp rate limit + honeypot silent-drop (R23) + Brevo `duplicate_parameter` mapped to outcome:'duplicate' returning 202 anyway.

Playwright a11y spec for admin user detail page exists at museum-web/e2e/a11y/admin-user-tier.a11y.spec.ts (3 tests: en page, fr page, en page with confirm modal open). T1 not T3 — N9/AC8 corrective honored vs R3/R4/R2 recurrent late-a11y loop.

TierToggleButton.tsx — confirm modal uses role='dialog' aria-modal='true' aria-labelledby='tier-modal-title'. Error message has role='alert' (line 111). Focus management: confirmRef autofocus on open (line 51-53). Cancel + confirm buttons both `disabled={busy}`. Good a11y baseline.

MINOR — TierToggleButton.tsx button lacks `aria-busy` during async submission. Spec mentions 'aria-busy + confirm modal a11y' in axis. `disabled={busy}` is sufficient for screen readers, but explicit `aria-busy={busy}` would be ideal. Not a blocker; backlog.

QuotaUpsellModal a11y: explicit accessibilityLabel on close (line 100), email field (line 119), consent checkbox (line 139), accessibilityRole='checkbox' + accessibilityState (line 140-141). Honeypot field has accessibilityElementsHidden + importantForAccessibility='no'. RTL-correct: `marginEnd: 8` (logical-side prop) per `feedback_rtl_start_end`. accessibilityRole='alert' on the card (line 95) — but unconditional alert role is slightly noisy (always announces).

MINOR — QuotaUpsellModal success/error states have no `accessibilityLiveRegion` (RN equivalent of aria-live). Status changes won't auto-announce to TalkBack/VoiceOver mid-flow. Acceptable for V1 modal; backlog.

MINOR — Spec R27 requires auto-close after 2 seconds on success. Implementation just renders the success message without setTimeout-driven close. The modal stays open in success state. Spec deviation, but UX-debatable (auto-close can frustrate users who want to read the success). Not a hard blocker; flag for spec confirmation.

Design tokens: TierToggleButton uses `bg-primary-50`, `text-text-primary`, `bg-surface-muted` — Tailwind theme tokens (good). QuotaUpsellModal uses a local `palette = {...}` const at module level (line 171-180) with HSL literals — not pulled from design-system tokens. Comment acknowledges this: 'V1.1 will move these to the shared theme tokens once the modal is themed'. Acceptable scoped deviation; backlog.

No unicode emoji anywhere in features/paywall, paywall.json locales, TierToggleButton. `feedback_no_unicode_emoji` honored. The close button uses '×' (multiplication sign), not 🗙 — acceptable typographic character.
RGPD checkbox: consent default `false` (line 61) + submit blocked until checked (line 68). N6 honored — explicit opt-in.
Dismiss patterns: close button (line 98-106) + onRequestClose handler on Modal (line 93) → hardware back on Android + swipe-down on iOS. Q7 dismissibility honored.

RBAC defense-in-depth: route (admin.route.ts:125) uses `requireRole('super_admin')`. Use case ChangeUserTierUseCase doesn't re-check role (route is authoritative). FE TierToggleButton.tsx:59-65 short-circuits to read-only label when viewerRole !== 'super_admin' — defense-in-depth FE hint, BE 403s independently. R14/R15/R32 honored.

Audit ordering: ChangeUserTierUseCase.execute (line 50-66) runs `await this.repository.changeUserTier(...)` THEN `await auditService.log({...})` THEN returns `updated`. Spec N3 says 'emit BEFORE returning 200' (post-mutation, pre-response) — honored. Note: review brief says 'audit log BEFORE mutation' which is NOT spec text; spec actually says pre-response, which is correct.
Idempotent flip skip: `if (previous.tier === input.newTier) return previous` (line 46) — no audit row emitted on no-op. Prevents double-click audit spam. Audit chain growth bounded.

Anti-enumeration on /paywall-interest: byIp rate limiter at 5 req/600s (paywallInterestLimiter, leads.route.ts:42), honeypot silent-drop (R23 — submitPaywallInterest.useCase.ts:48-54), Brevo duplicate_parameter mapped to outcome:'duplicate' → 202 (notifier.ts:66-72). Returns 202 regardless of brevoOutcome (route.ts:127). Idempotent 202 contract honored.
PII discipline: submitPaywallInterest.useCase.ts:76 logs `paywall_email_captured` with `requestId + emailDomain + brevoOutcome` only. Full email never logged. R21/N17 honored.
Mobile breadcrumb data: PaywallProvider.tsx:69 and QuotaUpsellModal.tsx:82 emit Sentry breadcrumbs with NO email payload — just category/type/message + reason metadata. No email-in-breadcrumb leak.

No new feature flag: only `FREE_TIER_MONTHLY_SESSION_LIMIT=3` config var added. Doctrine `feedback_no_feature_flags_prelaunch` honored. Disabling paywall = revert R1, not flag toggle (env.example comment explicit).

PII Risk7 — sessions_month_count is GDPR Art.4(1) personal data. Spec Risk7 acknowledges this and notes DSAR extension is a 'T3 follow-up, NOT a separate spec'. R1 commit does NOT include the DSAR extension. Spec self-flags; acceptable defer; tracked.

BLOCKER — Honesty UFR-013 partial fail: green-agent declared '1 web test failing' but `pnpm test` web actually reports 2 failed files (Footer.test.tsx + user-detail-tier-wire.test.tsx). The Footer one IS pre-existing (R1 didn't touch the file), but green-agent's report should have mentioned 2 failures and noted Footer as pre-existing baseline noise, not silently omitted it. Minor honesty gap; not malicious but incomplete.

BLOCKER — Honesty UFR-013: green-agent claimed migration governance was 'skipped (no local Docker DB) — testcontainer covers' — verified, add-user-tier.test.ts uses testcontainer and is GREEN. Honest declaration.
BLOCKER — Honesty UFR-013: green-agent claimed only `FREE_TIER_MONTHLY_SESSION_LIMIT` was added to .env.example. Verified VERBATIM via diff — no other env additions, no flags. Honest declaration.
Zero `as any` / `@ts-ignore` / `eslint-disable` in R1 source code (features/paywall, changeUserTier.useCase, submitPaywallInterest.useCase, monthly-session-quota.middleware, TierToggleButton). Clean.

blockers: AC15 violated — `pnpm lint` BE exits with code 2. File: museum-backend/tests/integration/security/auth-email-service-kind-prod-reject.test.ts:44 (literal block ends line 195). The new `AppEnv.freeTierMonthlySessionLimit` field (added at env.types.ts:319 + env.ts:289) is missing from the inline production-env stub. Existing sibling `freeTierDailyChatLimit: 100` lives at line 98. One-line fix: add `freeTierMonthlySessionLimit: 3,` adjacent. TS2741 error: 'Property freeTierMonthlySessionLimit is missing in type ... but required in type AppEnv.' Direct R1 regression; spec AC15 explicitly requires lint BE PASS.
niceToHave: Add `aria-busy={busy}` to TierToggleButton.tsx confirm + main button for explicit screen-reader state during async submission.
Add `accessibilityLiveRegion="polite"` (Android) + iOS announceForAccessibility shim to QuotaUpsellModal success/error <Text> nodes so screen readers auto-announce state transitions.
Implement spec R27 auto-close after 2s on success (or amend spec to confirm the manual-close UX is intentional — auto-close has UX debate around denying users time to read).
Move QuotaUpsellModal `palette` const into design-system tokens once mobile tokens consolidate (V1.1).
Consider strengthening AdminUserDTO type to mandate `tier: UserTier` rather than relying on `AdminUserWithTier` extension cast in TierToggleButton.tsx:42.
DSAR extension follow-up (Risk7) — add tier/sessionsMonthCount/sessionsMonthStart to `/api/users/me/export` shape before launch V1 bake completes.

Test-infra cleanup: extend __tests__/helpers/test-utils.tsx mock for `@sentry/react-native` with `addBreadcrumb: jest.fn()` so paywall breadcrumb assertions can run without the production safeAddBreadcrumb shim — would unblock the 2 mobile test failures without touching prod code.
Test-infra cleanup: add `<Suspense>` wrapper in user-detail-tier-wire.test.tsx Providers component to unblock the runtime assertion (the static-source assertion already passes).
Pre-existing Footer.test.tsx failure on main — not R1 territory but worth a chore commit before launch.

First it() block (static-source assertion) PASSES; only the runtime render fails. Test-infra issue, not implementation bug. Fix via Suspense wrapper backlog.
selfDeclaredFailuresVerdict.mobileTestPaywallBreadcrumbs: ACCEPTABLE_DEFER — verified: __tests__/helpers/test-utils.tsx:132-136 mocks @sentry/react-native without addBreadcrumb. Production Sentry exports addBreadcrumb normally → breadcrumbs DO fire in prod. Test-infra mock chain pattern issue, fixable by widening helper mock. Not a functional bug.
selfDeclaredFailuresVerdict.migrationGovernance: ACCEPTABLE — verified: museum-backend/tests/integration/db/migrations/add-user-tier.test.ts uses testcontainer for live schema assertion, PASSES in R1 BE suite. Local-Docker-DB skip is honest; coverage is via testcontainer integration test.

anyClaimsContradicted: Green-agent said '1 web test failing' but `pnpm test` actually shows 2 web test files failing — Footer.test.tsx (pre-existing on main, R1 didn't touch the file) + user-detail-tier-wire.test.tsx (acceptable defer).
